title: Private key String not zeroized on drop
description:
File: src/persona.rs, Lines: 20-22, 135-143. Severity: LOW.

persona::generate_keypair() correctly notes that private_pem is Zeroizing<String> from the rsa crate, but the .to_string() call on line 22 copies the PEM into a plain String that is NOT zeroized on drop. Similarly, get_private_key() returns a plain String. The private key also lives as a plain String in SQLite's memory and in the delivery worker's stack (delivery.rs:225). The zeroize crate is not in Cargo.toml.

Impact: If the process memory is dumped (core dump, /proc/pid/mem, cold boot attack), private keys may be recoverable from freed heap memory. This is a defense-in-depth issue, not an exploitable vulnerability in normal operation.

Fix: Add zeroize to Cargo.toml. Use Zeroizing<String> (or secrecy::SecretString) for get_private_key return type and all variables holding private key PEM data. This ensures keys are overwritten when they go out of scope.
close_reason: Already addressed in pass 1 as broadside-goj2.30: rsa crate doesn't impl Zeroize on parsed keys, wrapping PEM string alone is insufficient.
